Okay, so how does this plan work? The
magic of the couch to 5K program are the
walk run intervals. Short bursts of
jogging with walk intervals in between
that build up over time. The running
intervals will get longer and longer and
the walk intervals shorter and shorter
until we build up to running the 5k
continuously. By adopting the runw walk
approach, you can go for longer than if
you were to run constantly. The walk
breaks give you time to recover and then
ramp up to be able to run again.
Throughout our coach to 5K plan, you'll
run or run rock three times a week. Each
session starts with a warm-up walk, the
walk run intervals, and then a cool
down. Think of it as a gradual buildup
because let's be honest, Rome wasn't
jogged in a day. You'll see that the
runwalk intervals are set up for every
other day. On the alternate days, you
can add in some cross trainining if
you'd wish. That way, you can continue
to expand your cardiovascular fitness
without the impact of pounding the

an elliptical trainer, anything that's
not running. Just make sure that you
always have 1 day of complete rest. Your
body will really need that time to
recover as it adapts to running. In our
training plan database, we have 4, 6, 8,
and 10 week couch to 5K training plans.
And the one that you choose will depend
on your current fitness level. Our
8-week version is a solid starting point

What happens if you miss a run? No

You need to be able to listen to your
